Add-on Treatment.evidence: We assessed whether concomitant treatment with ADT plus docetaxel would result in longer overall survival than that with ADT alone.
Add-on Treatment.plan_extraction_plan: The study investigates "Chemohormonal Therapy in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer". The abstract states: "We assessed whether concomitant treatment with ADT plus docetaxel would result in longer overall survival than that with ADT alone." Therefore, the add-on treatment is docetaxel.

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.evidence: The total number of patients experiencing any event is 65 (16.7%) for Grade 3, 49 (12.6%) for Grade 4, and 1 (0.3%) for Grade 5.
Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.plan_extraction_plan: Table 3 provides a list of Grade 3 or higher adverse events among the 390 patients who received the docetaxel-containing regimen. The table lists the number and percentage of patients for each specific adverse event. To get the total, sum the number of patients experiencing each Grade 3 or higher event. For example, 7 (1.8%) had allergic reaction Grade 3, 1 (0.3%) had allergic reaction Grade 4, etc. The total number of patients experiencing any event is 65 (16.7%) for Grade 3, 49 (12.6%) for Grade 4, and 1 (0.3%) for Grade 5.

The article states "Adverse events among patients assigned to ADT alone were not routinely documented, although major adverse events were to be reported." Therefore, a comprehensive list of all Grade 3 or higher adverse events in the control arm is not available.

Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.evidence: The total number of patients experiencing any event is 65 (16.7%) for Grade 3, 49 (12.6%) for Grade 4, and 1 (0.3%) for Grade 5.
Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.plan_extraction_plan: Table 3 provides a list of Grade 3 or higher adverse events among the 390 patients who received the docetaxel-containing regimen. The table lists the number and percentage of patients for each specific adverse event. The attribution of relatedness to study therapy was made by the local investigators. To get the total, sum the number of patients experiencing each Grade 3 or higher event. For example, 7 (1.8%) had allergic reaction Grade 3, 1 (0.3%) had allergic reaction Grade 4, etc. The total number of patients experiencing any event is 65 (16.7%) for Grade 3, 49 (12.6%) for Grade 4, and 1 (0.3%) for Grade 5.

The article states "Adverse events among patients assigned to ADT alone were not routinely documented, although major adverse events were to be reported." Therefore, a comprehensive list of treatment-related Grade 3 or higher adverse events in the control arm is not available.

Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.evidence: 1 (0.3%) patient experienced a Grade 5 adverse event (sudden death) in the treatment arm, which was considered possibly related to docetaxel.
Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.plan_extraction_plan: Table 3 indicates that 1 (0.3%) patient experienced a Grade 5 adverse event (sudden death) in the treatment arm, which was considered possibly related to docetaxel.

The article states "Adverse events among patients assigned to ADT alone were not routinely documented, although major adverse events were to be reported." Therefore, a comprehensive list of treatment-related Grade 5 adverse events in the control arm is not available.

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.plan_extraction_plan: This column represents an overall risk judgment, which is not explicitly reported in this PDF. The PDF presents data and results, but doesn't include an assessment of the risk of bias or other factors that would contribute to an overall risk judgment.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel (at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area every 3 weeks for six cycles) or ADT alone.
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.chunk_id: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1::1
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.plan_extraction_plan: The abstract states "We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel (at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area every 3 weeks for six cycles) or ADT alone." Therefore, Treatment Arm 1 is ADT plus docetaxel. ADT is Androgen-deprivation therapy, which is a hormonal therapy. Docetaxel is a chemotherapy agent. Therefore, the Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1 is "Chemotherapy and Hormonal Therapy".

Control Arm.evidence: We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel... or ADT alone.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: Figure 1B (page 6) shows Kaplan-Meier estimates of overall survival for patients with high-volume disease. Table 1 (page 5) shows the number of patients with high vs low volume metastases at baseline.
Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.plan_extraction_plan: Yes. The study prospectively stratified patients according to high or low volume of metastatic disease. Figure 1B (page 6) shows Kaplan-Meier estimates of overall survival for patients with high-volume disease. Table 1 (page 5) shows the number of patients with high vs low volume metastases at baseline. Figure 2 (page 7) shows hazard ratios for death in subgroups, including high vs low volume.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: Figure 1C (page 6) shows Kaplan-Meier estimates of overall survival for patients with low-volume disease. Table 1 (page 5) shows the number of patients with high vs low volume metastases at baseline.
Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.plan_extraction_plan: Yes. The study prospectively stratified patients according to high or low volume of metastatic disease. Figure 1C (page 6) shows Kaplan-Meier estimates of overall survival for patients with low-volume disease. Table 1 (page 5) shows the number of patients with high vs low volume metastases at baseline. Figure 2 (page 7) shows hazard ratios for death in subgroups, including high vs low volume.

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: The median time to biochemical, symptomatic, or radiographic progression was 20.2 months in the combination group, as compared with 11.7 months in the ADT-alone group (hazard ratio, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.51 to 0.72; P<0.001). The rate of a prostate-specific antigen level of less than 0.2 ng per milliliter at 12 months was 27.7% in the combination group versus 16.8% in the ADT-alone group (P<0.001).

The rate of a prostate-specific antigen level of less than 0.2 ng per milliliter at 12 months was 27.7% in the combination group versus 16.8% in the ADT-alone group (P<0.001).

Type of Therapy.value: ADT plus docetaxel (at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area every 3 weeks for six cycles), ADT alone
Type of Therapy.evidence: We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel (at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area every 3 weeks for six cycles) or ADT alone.
